overview: A two-tier task management system comprising a Go backend REST API service handling authentication, tasks, categories, and due dates, and a React frontend web application providing a responsive UI. The frontend communicates with the backend via HTTPS/JSON, with session-based authentication using secure cookies.
requirements: Users can register and log in with email/password (min 8 chars, bcrypt cost≥10)
Lock account for 15 minutes after 3 failed login attempts
Sessions valid for 24 hours with logout support
Authenticated CRUD on tasks (title, description, due date, category, status)
Unique task IDs with creation and completion timestamps
Custom categories per user with unique names; deletion unassigns from tasks
Filter tasks by category and due-date range; default chronological order
Due dates in YYYY-MM-DD, overdue flagging, days-remaining display
Strict user data isolation; input validation/sanitization
CRUD responses <500ms; support 100 concurrent users and 10k tasks/user
Responsive UI with keyboard nav and <200ms visual feedback

components.name: task-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /auth/register:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [email, password]
              properties:
                email: {type: string}
                password: {type: string}
      responses:
        '201': {description: Created}
        '400': {description: Invalid}
  /auth/login:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                email: {type: string}
                password: {type: string}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
        '401': {description: Unauthorized}
        '423': {description: Locked}
  /auth/logout:
    post:
      responses:
        '204': {description: Logged out}
  /auth/password-reset:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                email: {type: string}
      responses:
        '200': {description: Email sent}
  /auth/password-reset/confirm:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                token: {type: string}
                newPassword: {type: string}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /tasks:
    get:
      parameters:
        - {name: categoryId, in: query, schema: {type: string}}
        - {name: dueFrom, in: query, schema: {type: string}}
        - {name: dueTo, in: query, schema: {type: string}}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [title]
              properties:
                title: {type: string}
                description: {type: string}
                dueDate: {type: string}
                categoryId: {type: string}
      responses:
        '201': {description: Created}
  /tasks/{id}:
    put:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: {type: object}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    delete:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '204': {description: Deleted}
  /tasks/{id}/complete:
    post:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /tasks/{id}/incomplete:
    post:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /categories:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [name]
              properties:
                name: {type: string}
      responses:
        '201': {description: Created}
        '409': {description: Duplicate}
  /categories/{id}:
    put:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties: {name: {type: string}}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    delete:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '204': {description: Deleted}
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Implement a Go backend using net/http on port 9090. Use in-memory storage (maps guarded by sync.RWMutex) for users, sessions, tasks, categories, login-attempt counters, and password-reset tokens. Persistence is not required but structure code so a DB could replace the store.

Authentication: POST /auth/register validates email regex and password length>=8; hashes password with bcrypt cost 10 (golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt). POST /auth/login verifies credentials; track failed attempts per email — after 3 failures lock account for 15 minutes (return 423). On success, issue a random session token (32 bytes hex) set as HttpOnly Secure cookie 'session' with 24h expiry; store session->userId mapping with expiry. POST /auth/logout deletes session. Password reset: /auth/password-reset issues a token (log it to stdout in lieu of email); /auth/password-reset/confirm validates token and updates password.

Middleware: authRequired reads cookie, validates session, injects userId into context. All /tasks and /categories endpoints require auth and enforce user isolation (filter by userId on every op; return 404 if resource belongs to different user).

Tasks: fields id(uuid), userId, title, description, dueDate(YYYY-MM-DD, optional), categoryId(optional), completed(bool), createdAt, completedAt. GET /tasks supports filters categoryId, dueFrom, dueTo; default sort by createdAt asc. Compute daysRemaining and overdue in response. POST creates, PUT edits, DELETE removes. /complete and /incomplete toggle status and set/clear completedAt.

Categories: id, userId, name. Enforce unique name per user (409 on duplicate). DELETE unassigns categoryId from tasks. Allow tasks/categories to be null-category.

Input validation: trim strings, reject empty titles/names, validate date format. Sanitize to prevent injection (even though in-memory). CORS: allow origin from env FRONTEND_ORIGIN with credentials.

/health returns 200 {"status":"ok"}. Log unauthorized attempts to stderr. Use JSON everywhere. Return errors as {"error":"message"}.

components.name: task-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task Web UI
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Build a React + TypeScript SPA using Vite, served by nginx in a Docker image. Provide a responsive UI (CSS flex/grid, mobile breakpoints) with pages: Login, Register, Password Reset, Dashboard (task list), Categories. Use fetch with credentials:'include' to call the task-api backend; base URL from VITE_API_URL env injected at build or runtime via /config.js.

Dashboard shows task title, category, due date, days-remaining, completion checkbox; overdue tasks flagged red. Controls to create/edit/delete tasks, assign category, set due date (date input YYYY-MM-DD). Filter by category and due-date range. Category management page for CRUD + rename. Provide full keyboard navigation (tab order, Enter to submit, Esc to cancel modals) and visible focus rings.

Show inline error messages from API. Use optimistic UI with <200ms feedback (spinners/toasts). Include a /health route returning 200 (nginx static file or simple JSON). Add nginx config to proxy /api to backend if same-origin deployment needed; otherwise configure CORS on backend.

Dockerfile: multi-stage (node build → nginx:alpine). Listen on port 8080.
